# MexVisa Pro — Complete Immigration Services Guide

> Express immigration consulting in Riviera Maya, Mexico. Mexican residency in 4 days. 2,500+ visas approved since 2009. Languages: English, Spanish, Russian, Chinese.

## About MexVisa Pro

MexVisa Pro is a professional immigration consulting firm founded in 2009, based in Playa del Carmen, Quintana Roo, Mexico. We serve the entire Riviera Maya region including Cancún, Playa del Carmen, Tulum, and surrounding areas. With 2,500+ visas approved and a 100% success rate, we are the leading immigration consultancy in the region.

We provide full-service immigration consulting in four languages: English, Spanish, Russian, and Chinese. Our team handles everything from initial consultation through document preparation, INM submission, and card issuance.

## Temporary Residency (Residente Temporal)

Temporary residency in Mexico is a 1-4 year renewable visa for foreigners who want to live in Mexico for work, retirement, investment, or family reunion. With MexVisa Pro's express service, the process takes 4 days from document submission to card issuance.

### Requirements
- Valid passport with at least 6 months validity
- Proof of income (bank statements showing approximately $2,500 USD/month)
- Passport-sized photographs
- Completed application forms
- No criminal record

### Process
1. Free consultation via WhatsApp to assess eligibility
2. Document preparation and review by our team
3. INM (National Immigration Institute) submission
4. Biometrics appointment
5. Residency card issuance

## Permanent Residency (Residente Permanente)

Permanent residency grants indefinite stay in Mexico with full rights. Available after 4 years of temporary residency, through family ties to a Mexican citizen, or by meeting financial requirements.

### Pathways
- Upgrade from temporary residency after 4 consecutive years
- Marriage to a Mexican citizen (after 2 years of temporary residency)
- Parent of a Mexican-born child
- Financial solvency (higher income threshold than temporary)
- Retirement with sufficient pension income

## Work Permits

Foreign nationals need explicit work authorization to work legally in Mexico. This can be added to a temporary or permanent residency card.

### Types
- Employer-sponsored work permit (company applies on your behalf)
- Freelance/independent work authorization
- Professional services permit
- Intra-company transfer

## Digital Nomad Visa

Mexico offers temporary residency for remote workers who earn income from abroad. This is ideal for freelancers, entrepreneurs, and employees of foreign companies who want to live in the Riviera Maya while working remotely.

### Requirements
- Proof of remote employment or freelance income
- Income of approximately $2,500 USD/month from foreign sources
- Valid passport
- No intention to seek local employment

## Investor & Business Visas

For entrepreneurs and investors looking to start or invest in a business in Mexico. Requirements include proof of investment capital and a viable business plan.

## Family Reunification

Mexico offers family reunification visas for spouses, dependent children, and parents of residents or citizens. The primary applicant's immigration status determines eligibility.

## Mexican Citizenship & Naturalization

After 5 years of legal residency (2 years if married to a Mexican citizen), foreigners can apply for Mexican citizenship through naturalization.

### Requirements
- 5 years of continuous legal residency (2 years with Mexican spouse)
- Spanish language proficiency (basic conversational level)
- Knowledge of Mexican history and culture (naturalization exam)
- No criminal record
- Proof of economic self-sufficiency

### Process Timeline
- Application submission to SRE (Secretaría de Relaciones Exteriores)
- Language and culture examination
- Document verification
- Citizenship ceremony
- Total processing time: approximately 6-12 months

## Service Area

- Cancún, Quintana Roo
- Playa del Carmen, Quintana Roo
- Tulum, Quintana Roo
- Puerto Morelos, Quintana Roo
- Isla Mujeres, Quintana Roo
- Cozumel, Quintana Roo
- Entire Riviera Maya region
